summary.one_line: Theta Gold Mines presents strong growth and production roadmap at the 121 Mining Investment Conference.
key_points: Targeting 1.06Moz over 13.1 years, producing 90-120koz p.a.
Current gold resource is 6.1Moz at 4.17g/t.
Projected NPV at spot price (US$2,884/oz) is US$455 million post-tax.
Completed front-end engineering design (FEED) for processing plant.
Plant construction underway with commissioning targeted for Q4 2026.
Equity funding of US$30 million raised; US$80 million debt facility is non-binding but executed.
Rapid payback at 29 months from start of mining operations.
Production expected to commence Q1 2027 after securing all necessary financing.
Existing permits and mining rights for all four mines in proximity to processing plant.
Exploration potential through 40 nearby historic mines for future development.
AISC estimated at US$1,181/oz, positioning TGM among the lowest-cost producers.
Proven, scalable processing techniques with near-term upside from surface dumps.
First gold from surface dump production expected in Q1 2027.
Strategic initiatives aligned with ESG standards for community and environmental impact.
Targeted expansion phase for modular plant design to increase production capacity.

Post-tax IRR: 77%; Pre-tax IRR: 84%.
First gold production scheduled for Q1 2027.
Free cash flow estimated at AUD 1.4 billion over project life.
Average gold recovery: 4.28 g/t with 86.2% recovery rate.
Life of Mine (LOM): 13.1 years with total gold mined of 1.01 million oz.
Total operating cost of USD 139.5 per ounce.
Peak funding requirement of USD 77 million (AUD 116 million).
Production ramping up to 45 ktpm across four mines.
Development capital costs include initial capital of AUD 154.2 million.
AISC: USD 1,181/oz (AUD 1,787/oz).
Operating costs averaged USD 139.5/tonne.
Development includes infrastructure upgrades at historical mining sites.
Inferred resources provide potential for increased future production.
Strong leverage to gold prices with project economics highly sensitive to price fluctuations.
Project area includes historical mines: Beta, Rietfontein, Frankfort, and CDM.
Contractual agreements in place for initial construction phases.
Part of broader ESG strategy with focus on sustainability and community engagement.
Estimated 29-month payback period from the start of mining.
Independent mining engineer's review supports feasibility metrics.

summary.one_line: Theta Gold Mines secures US$80 million non-binding funding agreement to support TGME project.
key_points: Non-binding Senior Term Loan for US$80 million (~A$120 million).
Funding by Nebari Partners in two tranches: Tranche 1 of US$45 million and Tranche 2 of up to US$35 million.
Tranche 1 available at closing in early 2026, Tranche 2 subject to conditions.
Interest at 3-month SOFR plus 10% per annum, with a floor of 3.75%.
12 months interest capitalisation and 18-month moratorium on principal repayments.
Five-year term from Tranche 1 draw-down.
Funding aimed at completing construction and achieving first gold pour in Q1 2027.
Signed term sheet allows for potential co-lending with other lenders.
Facility fully secures Theta Gold's and subsidiaries' assets.
Warrants for 27% of funded amount included, exercisable at a 40% premium.
Conditions for funding include successful due-diligence and approval from Nebari's investment committee.
Completion of previous equity funding agreements required before closing.
Funding represents a significant step towards the TGME Gold Mine Project.
Project located in Eastern Transvaal Goldfields, South Africa.
Expected gold prices above US$4,000/oz may influence project economics positively.
Management expresses confidence in meeting project milestones.
Announcement shows strong support from investors and notable partners.
Due diligence required before binding legal documentation is finalized.
Company will update the market in line with ASX Listing Rules.

achieve first gold pour in Q1 2027

catalysts_next_12m: Closing of the funding agreement after due diligence.
Completion of conditions precedent for Tranche 2.
risks_headwinds: Dependence on successful due diligence.
Market conditions affecting gold prices.

summary.market_impact_assessment: The funding agreement bolsters Theta Gold's financial position, potentially enhancing investor confidence and supporting project progression, with implications for production and revenue in a rising gold price environment.

price_sensitive.reason: The announcement relates to a capital raising activity through a Share Purchase Plan and options, which could impact share price.

summary.one_line: Theta Gold Mines announces a Share Purchase Plan and options offer to raise capital.
key_points: Theta Gold Mines opens a Share Purchase Plan (SPP Offer) and an Options Offer.
SPP Offer price set at $0.21 per share.
Eligible shareholders as of 6 October 2025 can participate.
Total amount raised in private placement prior to SPP is A$12 million.
Attaching Options provided: 1 option for every 2.38 shares subscribed.
Options exercisable at $0.32, expiring 18 months after grant.
SPP and Options Offers close at 5:00 PM AEDT on 17 November 2025.
Applications must be submitted before the closing date.
The offer is exclusive to select regions: Australia, New Zealand, UK, Singapore, Hong Kong.
The announcement is not an offer to the U.S. market due to regulatory restrictions.

timeline_milestones.milestone: Share Purchase Plan and Options Offer closing date
timeline_milestones.source_snippet: The SPP Offer and Options Offers close at 5:00PM (AEDT) on Monday, 17 November 2025.

capital_structure: Introduction of a Share Purchase Plan for raising equity.
Issuance of Attaching Options encouraging participation in capital raising.
catalysts_next_12m: Potential increase in equity base through shareholder participation in SPP.
Exercising of options, potentially enhancing capital structure.
risks_headwinds: Potential shareholder dilution from capital raising.
Regulatory risks in jurisdictions outside specified regions.
summary.market_impact_assessment: The announcement of the capital raising could lead to a positive or negative reaction in the market, depending on investor sentiment and participation levels. Positive engagement in the SPP might support share price, while insufficient interest could lead to dilution concerns.

notes: All key financial figures and timelines provided are clear and directly relevant.

source_meta.title: Notice of Share Purchase Plan & Options Offer Prospectus
